channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Global geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: ☢️ Nuclear Weapons Spending Hits Record $119 Billion as Arms Race Intensifies
item.description: Global spending on nuclear weapons rose 19% in 2025 to $119 billion, the highest since tracking began. The US led with $69.2 billion, more than all other nuclear states combined, while China increased spending by 7% to $13.5 billion.

item.title: ⚖️ ICC Chief Prosecutor Karim Khan Suspended Over Sexual Misconduct Allegations
item.description: The International Criminal Court's oversight body suspended Chief Prosecutor Karim Khan late Monday, referring him for disciplinary proceedings. Khan faces allegations of sexual misconduct with a female aide spanning more than two years, which he denies.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇰🇵 Xi Jinping Visits North Korea for First Time in Seven Years, Pledges Strategic Cooperation
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping met with Kim Jong Un in Pyongyang, agreeing to expand bilateral cooperation. Kim pledged full support for the "One China principle" on Taiwan, while Xi called for deepening strategic coordination between the two nations.

item.title: 🤖 OpenAI Files Confidential SEC Paperwork for Potential IPO
item.description: ChatGPT maker OpenAI filed preliminary paperwork with the SEC that could lead to a Wall Street debut. The company said it may be "a while" before going public, but the filing gives it the option to move sooner if beneficial.

item.title: 🐄 Flesh-Eating Screwworm Parasite Spreads Beyond Texas, Canada Blocks Livestock Imports
item.description: The USDA confirmed three new screwworm cases including one outside Texas, in New Mexico. The flesh-eating parasite poses a threat to the cattle industry, with five confirmed cases so far including calves, a goat, and a dog.

item.title: 🇮🇷🇮🇱 Iran and Israel Halt Strikes After Trump Appeal
item.description: Iran and Israel said they halted attacks on each other after US President Donald Trump called for an immediate ceasefire, though Tehran warned it would resume strikes if Israel continued hitting Hezbollah in Lebanon.

item.title: 🇨🇳📦 China Exports Surge on Chip Demand and Front-Loaded Orders
item.description: China's export growth likely strengthened in May as companies rushed orders ahead of energy price pressures from the Gulf conflict, while global semiconductor demand remained robust, a Reuters poll showed.

item.title: 🇮🇳📈 India Inflation Rises to 4% on Food and Fuel Costs
item.description: India's inflation likely rose to the Reserve Bank's 4% target in May, driven by higher vegetable prices and fuel costs linked to the US-Israel conflict with Iran, a Reuters poll indicated.

item.title: ⚔️🛑 Iran and Israel Pull Back After Fierce Exchange of Attacks
item.description: The conflict between Israel and Hezbollah, an Iranian-backed militia, is complicating efforts to end the war in Iran as both sides stepped back from further escalation following intense strikes.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China Begins Large-Scale Delivery of Gallium Chips for 6G Network
item.description: Beijing is deploying millions of third-generation semiconductors using silicon-based technology for a planned space-ground 6G communications network, advancing its strategic tech capabilities amid export controls.

item.title: 🇹🇼 Taiwan Hits Back at Beijing's "Cognitive Warfare" After Coastguard Patrols
item.description: Taiwan's defense minister accused Beijing of "cognitive warfare" after Chinese coastguard vessels patrolled waters around the island, saying the operation aimed to assert jurisdiction over areas Beijing does not control.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Russia Rejects Ukrainian, European Peace Initiatives, Says Battlefield Will Decide War
item.description: Senior Russian officials effectively rejected recent Ukrainian and European proposals aimed at restarting peace talks, signaling that Moscow sees military gains as the path to resolution.

item.title: 🇪🇺 Polish Concerns Over Ukraine EU Entry "Solved," Enlargement Chief Says
item.description: The EU's enlargement chief Marta Kos said concerns raised by Poland over Ukraine's EU accession have been addressed at the working level, potentially smoothing the path for Kyiv's membership bid.

item.title: 🇹🇷 NATO's Ankara Summit: A Reckoning for the Alliance
item.description: As NATO allies confront uncertainties on multiple fronts, the upcoming Ankara Summit may become the alliance's most important meeting of the decade, with Turkey playing host amid shifting regional dynamics.

item.title: ⛪ Pope Leo XIV warns of 'profound crisis' in historic address to Spanish parliament
item.description: In his first speech to a national parliament, Pope Leo XIV called for respect for migrants and international law, warning that escalating conflicts and polarization have pushed the world into a deep crisis.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China's Xi vows unwavering support for North Korea in rare Pyongyang visit
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping made a rare visit to North Korea, pledging to safeguard common interests and fight 'hegemony' alongside Kim Jong Un, signaling deepening ties amid regional tensions.

item.title: 🇵🇭 Magnitude 7.8 earthquake kills at least 35 in southern Philippines
item.description: A powerful 7.8-magnitude earthquake struck off Mindanao island, killing at least 35 people and injuring dozens. Buildings collapsed in General Santos City as authorities launched search and rescue operations.

item.title: 📱 Apple unveils AI-powered Siri overhaul at WWDC 2026
item.description: Apple announced a major revamp of its voice assistant at its annual developers conference, introducing 'Siri AI' with better voice recognition and a standalone app, marking the company's long-awaited push into generative AI.

item.title: 🦠 WHO urges Uganda to reconsider Congo border closure over Ebola outbreak
item.description: The WHO chief called on Uganda to reconsider closing its border with DR Congo, where confirmed Ebola cases have risen to 515 with over 100 deaths. Armed groups threaten response efforts.

item.title: 🇱🇧 Lebanon says Israel has bombed it nearly 3,500 times during ceasefire
item.description: Lebanon defense minister says Israel carried out nearly 3,500 airstrikes and hundreds of controlled explosions since the US-announced ceasefire on April 16.

item.title: 🇹🇼 Taiwan calls Chinese Coast Guard patrols to its east provocative
item.description: Taiwan defense minister condemned Chinese Coast Guard patrols east of the island as provocative, vowing military coordination with the coast guard to respond.

item.title: 🇨🇳 Chinese military maneuvered near Taiwan as global tech executives gathered
item.description: A hostile maritime exchange with China developed as Nvidia, Intel and other tech leaders attended a major Taiwan tech showcase, underscoring the island strategic importance.

item.title: 🇰🇷 South Korea to unveil major investment project as AI boom swells tax revenue
item.description: President Lee Jae Myung announced plans for a large-scale investment project, citing buoyant tax revenues from the chip sector amid the AI boom.

item.title: 📉 Gulf stock markets drop as Middle East tensions weigh on investor sentiment
item.description: Most Gulf stock markets ended lower on Monday as renewed conflict in the Middle East rattled investors despite ceasefire talks between Iran and Israel.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Bennett slams Netanyahu for "normalizing" Iran attacks
item.description: Former Israeli PM Naftali Bennett criticized Netanyahu for accepting periodic Iranian strikes as routine, arguing Israel should not become accustomed to being attacked.
